title: Roomio Camera Path
description: The hand-off artifact between the flythrough authoring stage (waypoints or recorded walk) and the playback/record stage. An ordered list of control points the camera flies through, plus per-point look-at + dwell and global timing/fps. Saved/loaded as JSON so a path is reusable and shareable. Published by Agent B (camera-flythrough); the front-end may also save/load it alongside a RoomDesign.

version.description: Schema version. Additive fields only within 1.x; breaking changes bump the major and are announced in roomio.txt before landing.

name.description: Human label for the path, e.g. 'Entry walkthrough'.

designId.description: Optional id of the RoomDesign this path was authored against. A path is most meaningful replayed in the scene it was drawn in; null = not bound to a specific design.

coordinateSpace.description: All position/lookAt vectors are in Three.js scene units: METERS, with the origin at the room's bounding-box center (matching src/three/coords.ts makeFrame: worldX = (designCm.x - bboxCenterCm.x)/100). Stored in world space so the recording camera consumes the values directly with no conversion. The room only re-centers when its corner polygon (shape) changes, so a path stays valid as long as the room shape is unchanged.

fps.description: Frames per second for deterministic frame-by-frame capture (F6). Playback preview (F5) is time-based and independent of this.

duration.description: Total travel time in SECONDS for one pass along the curve at constant arc-length speed (excludes dwell pauses). Total clip length = duration + sum(controlPoints[].dwell).

loop.description: If true, the path is treated as a closed loop (CatmullRomCurve3 closed) and playback wraps t: 1 -> 0.

eyeHeight.description: Default camera height in meters when a control point omits an explicit y (e.g. points authored by clicking the floor in the top-down view). Recorded-walk points carry their own y.

lookAheadEps.description: Fraction of the curve to look ahead for smooth turning when a control point has no explicit lookAt: lookTarget = curve.getPointAt(min(t + lookAheadEps, 1)). Fewer control points + a small look-ahead = the camera anticipates bends instead of snapping.

fov.description: Vertical field of view (degrees) of the recording camera during playback/capture.

controlPoints.description: Ordered waypoints the Catmull-Rom curve passes THROUGH (interpolating spline, not Bezier). Authored by dropping/dragging waypoints (F3) or by decimating a recorded walk (F4). Order defines travel direction.

position.description: [x, y, z] camera position in world meters. y is eye height (defaults to eyeHeight when authored on the floor plane).

lookAt.description: Optional explicit [x, y, z] world-meter target the camera aims at while passing this point. null/absent => use curve look-ahead (lookAheadEps).

dwell.description: Seconds to pause (hold position, optionally easing the look-at) at this control point during playback. Adds to total clip length beyond `duration`.
